metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5049
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1,2: yeast two-hybrid and co-immunoprecipitation in HEK293T cells showed α-Actinin-1 (ACTN1) interacts with GluR4 (GRIA4).

Figure 3:
"Myc-tagged full-length α-Actinin-1 and IQGAP1 clones were transfected into low density hippocampal primary neuronal cultures together with GFP-tagged GluR4, and the cellular localization of these proteins was analyzed by immunocytochemistry 2 days after transfection.

GFP-GluR4, myc-α-Actinin-1 and myc-IQGAP1 all showed punctate signals throughout the dendrite, both in the shafts and the putative spines (Fig. 3). Moreover, clusters of GFP-GluR4 overlapped extensively with those of myc-α-Actinin-1 (Fig. 3a) and myc-IQGAP1 (Fig. 3b). Co-localizations patterns were even clearer with the surface population of GFP-GluR4 that was specifically labeled by live staining."

Figure 4f: GRIA4 and ACTN1 co-localized in hippocampal neuronal cultures that were double-labeled with anti-GluR4 and anti-α-Actinin.. Experimental description: Although not explicitly mentioned, rat species was used throughout the paper. e.g. in pulldowns; "Moreover, co-immunoprecipitation experiments from rat cerebellar P2 extracts using an anti-GluR4 antiserum confirmed the interaction between α-Actinin-1 and GluR4 in brain (Fig. 2c)."

antibody;
Anti-α-Actinin antibody (Sigma, St Louis, MO, USA and Santa Cruz Biotechnology, Santa Cruz, CA, USA)
